detail: test
Design system + 15 task designs
Building marketing strategy
Implement Clerk Authentication with Multi-Tenant Data Isolation
Implement Stripe Checkout & Webhook-Driven Subscription Management
Build Admin Analytics Dashboard with Event Tracking & Conversion Metrics
Build SEO-Optimized Landing Pages with Structured Data and Analytics
Implement SendGrid Email Drip Campaign with Vercel Cron

Using recharts for charts (lightweight, Next.js-friendly), better-sqlite3 for sync DB access in server components, Anthropic SDK with streaming for real-time AI responses (prefer over OpenAI for structured reasoning on tax optimization), SHA-256 context hashing to dedupe recommendations, Vercel AI SDK for streaming utilities. FTC carryforward follows CRA T2209 10-year carryback/carryforward rules. AI recommendations use Claude Sonnet 4.6 for cost efficiency (~$3/1M input tokens) vs Opus.
Creating design specs

Using better-sqlite3 transactions for atomicity, Zod for CSV validation (type-safe + runtime checks), react-dropzone for modern file UX, Papa Parse for robust CSV parsing. Row-level security via middleware HOC pattern rather than database triggers (easier to debug in SQLite). Organization invite flow uses time-limited JWT tokens in email links. No ORMs to keep SQLite queries explicit and performant.

Next.js 14 App Router with server components for SEO pages, Vercel Cron for email automation (simpler than external scheduler), SendGrid Dynamic Templates for email management, SQLite with better-sqlite3 for referral/affiliate tracking, Stripe metadata for referral attribution, nanoid for collision-resistant referral codes, Stripe Connect deferred to v2 (manual payouts initially)

Clerk for auth (better DX than NextAuth for App Router, built-in <UserButton/>), Stripe Checkout (not Payment Links) for custom metadata passing, better-sqlite3 for analytics (fast local queries, simple migration path to PostgreSQL if needed), recharts over Chart.js (treeshakes better with Next.js), admin role via Clerk publicMetadata (avoids separate RBAC table), webhook-driven subscription updates (source of truth is Stripe, not polling)

**Core Stack:** Next.js 14 App Router (server components for data fetching, client components for interactivity), TypeScript strict mode, TailwindCSS for styling. **Tax Engine:** Pure TypeScript functions in /lib/tax/ following IRS Form 1116 and CRA T2209 rules with Article XV treaty allocation. **Visualization:** Recharts for responsive charts (lightweight, React-native, good mobile support). **PDF Generation:** jsPDF + jspdf-autotable (client-side generation avoids server memory issues, faster for single-user MVP). **Testing:** Vitest for unit tests on tax calculations (critical for accuracy). **Database:** Assuming SQLite with better-sqlite3 based on project brief. **Type Safety:** All tax functions return strongly-typed interfaces to prevent calculation errors downstream.

Next.js 15 App Router with Server Components for data fetching, Server Actions for mutations (form completion), optimistic UI updates for instant feedback. SQLite with better-sqlite3 (synchronous queries in Server Components). Radix UI for accessible accordion (WAI-ARIA compliant). Bank of Canada Valet API with 24-hour cache TTL. TypeScript strict mode with explicit types for all data structures. Tailwind CSS for styling with mobile-first responsive design.

**Tech Stack:** Next.js 14+ App Router for server components and API routes, TypeScript for type safety, TailwindCSS + shadcn/ui for consistent fintech-style UI with pre-built accessible components (Form, Card, Badge, Progress). SQLite via better-sqlite3 for local-first data persistence (no external DB required for MVP). Zod + react-hook-form for robust client-side validation. Vitest for fast unit testing of tax calculation logic.

**Architecture:** Server components for data fetching (RSU tax page), API routes for CRUD operations and complex tax calculations, client components only where interactivity needed (form submission). Tax calculators are pure functions in `/lib/tax/` for easy testing and reusability. Separation of concerns: DB in `/lib/db/`, tax logic in `/lib/tax/`, UI in `/components/` and `/app/`.

**Key Decisions:**
1. **Hardcoded 1.35 USD/CAD rate for MVP** - Real Bank of Canada API integration deferred to post-MVP
2. **100% US-sourced income assumption** - Article XV proration implemented but defaults to 100% for H-1B/TN workers still in US (will add UI for partial-year residents later)
3. **Single filing status only** - Married filing jointly deferred to post-MVP
4. **No database migrations** - SQLite schema created via `db.exec()` on initialization, suitable for MVP
5. **Server-side tax calculations** - Keep sensitive logic server-side, send computed results to client
6. **shadcn/ui over custom components** - Faster development, better accessibility, consistent design system

Next.js 14.2+ App Router with React Server Components, TypeScript strict mode, shadcn/ui component library (New York style), SQLite via better-sqlite3 with WAL mode for concurrent reads, fintech dark theme (slate-900 bg + emerald/amber accents), tsx for script execution, CSS variables for theming, lucide-react for icons. Tax bracket data hardcoded from authoritative IRS/CRA sources (Rev. Proc. 2024-40, CRA 2025 tables) with JSDoc references. Progressive tax calculation using marginal bracket system. All three tasks are independent and can be executed in parallel.

Transform TaxBridge MVP into a revenue-generating SaaS product targeting 50K+ H-1B/TN visa tech workers at FAANG companies. Phase 1: Launch freemium model with Stripe payments and SEO-optimized landing pages. Phase 2: Build enterprise features for immigration law firms managing 100+ clients. Revenue target: $83K/month via $299/year individual subscriptions (280 users) + $2K/year enterprise seats (25 firms).
Refining tasks
Sprint 0 complete. All 14 tasks done. Starting next sprint.

Design system + 8 task designs
Initialize Next.js 14+ App Router project with shadcn/ui and fintech-grade styling
Implement SQLite database with better-sqlite3, schema migrations, and type-safe queries
Define TypeScript domain models and 2025 US/Canada tax bracket constants with CRA/IRS sources
Build RSU Entry Form with shadcn/ui Components
Implement US Federal + State Tax Calculator with 2025 IRS Brackets

Using jsPDF over alternatives (pdfmake, react-pdf) for simpler server-side generation without React component complexity. Recharts for FTC visualization provides responsive charts with minimal bundle size. All calculations server-side in API routes for security (no client-side tax logic exposure). SQLite queries via better-sqlite3 synchronous API for simplicity in Next.js route handlers. Treaty Article XV logic centralized in ftc-calculator.ts for reusability across UI and PDF export.

Next.js 15 App Router with Server Components for data fetching, better-sqlite3 for local database (already in use), Radix UI for accessible accordion component, Bank of Canada Valet API for live exchange rates with 24-hour caching strategy, TailwindCSS for styling (existing), TypeScript for type safety (existing). Database schema extends existing tables (users, rsu_events, tax_calculations) with exchange_rates and form_completion. MVP uses hardcoded user_id=1 to avoid auth complexity.

**Stack:** Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui (Radix UI primitives), better-sqlite3 (SQLite DB already initialized at `/lib/db/init.ts`), Zod validation (already in use), react-hook-form, Vitest for testing. **Architecture:** Server-side API routes for data fetching, server components for pages, client components for interactive forms. **Tax Logic:** Article XV treaty proration for US-sourced income, FTC calculation to prevent double taxation. **Exchange Rates:** Bank of Canada API via `/lib/currency.ts` or hardcoded 1.35 CAD/USD for MVP. **UI:** Modern fintech aesthetic with gradient accents (blue-600 to indigo-600), shadcn/ui components, responsive 2-column layout, color-coded tax brackets, animated progress bars.

Next.js 14+ App Router for modern React architecture with server components, better-sqlite3 for zero-config embedded database (no network overhead, ideal for MVP), shadcn/ui for production-ready accessible components, TypeScript strict mode for type safety on financial calculations, TailwindCSS for rapid fintech-grade UI development with dark mode support.

Build TaxBridge MVP in 4 phases: (1) establish Next.js foundation with SQLite database and TypeScript types, (2) implement core RSU entry and dual-country tax calculation engine using 2024/2025 tax brackets and treaty Article XV, (3) build user-facing dashboard with currency conversion and forms checklist, (4) add Foreign Tax Credit optimizer to eliminate double taxation. Prioritize working end-to-end flow over polish.
